title: Buttery Flaky Australian Steak Stir-fry
description: A low-sodium-friendly monsoon dish featuring fillet of steak stir-fried with a buttery, flaky texture and steamed vegetables for added freshness.
instructions: Season the steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place the steaks in a baking dish, drizzle with vegetable oil, and bake for 7-8 minutes per side until cooked through. Let rest for 5 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
Heat a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the vegetable oil and swirl to coat the bottom of the pan.
Add the onion, capsicum, broccoli, shredded carrot, garlic, and herbes de Provence powder to the pan. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.

Increase heat to high and add the sliced steak to the pan. Pour in the low-sodium soy sauce and squeeze half a lime over the top. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the beef is nicely caramelized and slightly crispy on the outside.
Remove from heat, garnish with lemon thyme sprigs if desired, and serve immediately.
